name: The Long Way
description: The figure turned. It was him. And the worst, best thing happened: in person, with all the time in the world, they had nothing to say. The four-minute magic was gone. So they had to learn each other the slow way, from zero.

divergence_event.timeline_ref: The figure turns — and it's him
divergence_event.description: He turns, and it's him. But the intimacy of the dead-spot windows doesn't survive contact with daylight and unlimited time. They have to start over, badly, in person.

images.narrative_caption: He turned, and it was him. And it was awful. Three feet apart, full signal, nothing to say.
A slightly shorter, more tired, more real person than the one she'd built from four-minute windows.
They knew each other's deepest fears and could not, for the life of them, figure out where to get breakfast.
Then, for a second or two, it caught. A real laugh. A real silence that didn't hurt.
She gave him a number that worked everywhere. Not just on the 23:40. Some things you learn the long way.
